medicines.name: Paracetamol (Crocin/Dolo 650)
medicines.use: Relieves mild to moderate headache and fever
medicines.dosage: 500mg–650mg every 4–6 hours. Max 4 doses/day for elderly.
medicines.warning: Do not exceed 2g/day if liver issues. Avoid alcohol.

medicines.name: Aspirin (Disprin)
medicines.use: Headache and mild pain relief
medicines.dosage: 325mg once after food. Use with caution in elderly.
medicines.warning: Avoid if on blood thinners or have stomach ulcers.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen (Brufen)
medicines.use: Anti-inflammatory, relieves tension headache
medicines.dosage: 200mg once after food. Use lowest dose for shortest time.
medicines.warning: Avoid in kidney disease, heart conditions. Take after food only.

headache.disclaimer: Frequent headaches may indicate BP issues. Consult a doctor if headache persists more than 2 days.

medicines.name: Paracetamol (Crocin/Dolo 650)
medicines.use: Reduces fever and body temperature
medicines.dosage: 650mg every 6 hours with water. Rest and hydrate well.
medicines.warning: If fever is above 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, visit hospital immediately.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen (Brufen 400)
medicines.use: Fever reduction and inflammation
medicines.dosage: 400mg after meals, twice daily
medicines.warning: Avoid in elderly with kidney problems. Always take after food.

fever.disclaimer: High fever in elderly can be dangerous. Seek medical attention if fever exceeds 103°F or persists beyond 2 days.

medicines.name: Diclofenac Gel (Voveran Gel)
medicines.use: Apply on knee for pain relief
medicines.dosage: Apply 2–3 times daily on affected area. Do not bandage tightly.
medicines.warning: For external use only. Avoid open wounds.

medicines.name: Paracetamol (Dolo 650)
medicines.use: Oral pain relief for arthritis knee pain
medicines.dosage: 650mg twice daily after meals
medicines.warning: Long-term use may affect liver. Use only as needed.

medicines.name: Calcium + Vitamin D3 (Shelcal)
medicines.use: Strengthens bones and reduces joint pain over time
medicines.dosage: 1 tablet daily after dinner
medicines.warning: Safe for elderly. Take regularly for 3+ months for best effect.

knee pain.disclaimer: Knee pain in elderly may indicate arthritis or calcium deficiency. Physiotherapy and warm fomentation also help greatly.

medicines.name: Diclofenac + Paracetamol (Voveran Plus)
medicines.use: Combined pain relief for back pain
medicines.dosage: 1 tablet after food, twice daily. Maximum 5 days.
medicines.warning: Do not use more than 5 days without doctor advice.

medicines.name: Muscle Relaxant – Thiocolchicoside (Myospaz)
medicines.use: Relieves muscle spasm in back
medicines.dosage: 4mg twice daily after meals, for 3–5 days only
medicines.warning: May cause drowsiness. Do not drive. Elderly should use with caution.

back pain.disclaimer: Back pain with numbness or radiating leg pain should be evaluated by a doctor. Rest, gentle stretching, and hot/cold packs also help.

medicines.name: Cetirizine (Zyrtec/Cetzine)
medicines.use: Relieves runny nose, sneezing, watery eyes
medicines.dosage: 10mg once at bedtime
medicines.warning: May cause drowsiness. Elderly should take at night only.

medicines.name: Paracetamol + Phenylephrine (Sinarest)
medicines.use: Cold, blocked nose, mild fever
medicines.dosage: 1 tablet twice daily after food
medicines.warning: Avoid if you have high BP. Contains decongestant.

medicines.name: Steam Inhalation
medicines.use: Natural relief for blocked nose and congestion
medicines.dosage: Inhale steam for 10 minutes, 2–3 times daily
medicines.warning: Be careful of hot water burns. Use a steam inhaler safely.

cold.disclaimer: Cold usually resolves in 7 days. If symptoms worsen with chest pain or high fever, consult a doctor.

medicines.name: Meclizine (Vertin)
medicines.use: Relieves dizziness and vertigo
medicines.dosage: 16mg twice daily. Take with water.
medicines.warning: May cause drowsiness. Sit or lie down when dizzy to prevent falls.

medicines.name: Betahistine (Betavert)
medicines.use: Reduces inner ear dizziness (Meniere's disease)
medicines.dosage: 8–16mg three times daily after meals
medicines.warning: Take regularly as prescribed. Effects take a few weeks.

dizziness.disclaimer: Dizziness in elderly can be due to low BP, dehydration, or inner ear issues. Drink water, stand up slowly. See a doctor if it's frequent.

medicines.name: Antacid (Digene/Gelusil)
medicines.use: Relieves acidity, gas, and mild stomach pain
medicines.dosage: 2 tablets after meals or when pain occurs
medicines.warning: Safe for short-term use. If pain is severe or recurring, see a doctor.

medicines.name: Domperidone (Domstal)
medicines.use: Relieves nausea and stomach discomfort
medicines.dosage: 10mg 30 minutes before meals
medicines.warning: Use for short periods only. Elderly should use low dose.

medicines.name: Omeprazole (Omez)
medicines.use: Reduces stomach acid, treats ulcer pain
medicines.dosage: 20mg once in the morning before breakfast
medicines.warning: For long-term acid issues. Consult doctor for use beyond 2 weeks.

stomach pain.disclaimer: Sudden severe stomach pain, especially with vomiting or black stools, requires immediate medical attention.

medicines.name: Metformin (Glycomet 500)
medicines.use: Controls blood sugar in Type 2 Diabetes
medicines.dosage: 500mg twice daily with meals as prescribed by doctor
medicines.warning: Do not skip meals when taking. Monitor blood sugar regularly.

medicines.name: Glimepiride (Amaryl)
medicines.use: Lowers blood sugar levels
medicines.dosage: 1–2mg before breakfast as prescribed
medicines.warning: Can cause low blood sugar (hypoglycemia). Always carry glucose tablets.

diabetes.disclaimer: Diabetes medicines must only be taken as prescribed by a doctor. Regular blood sugar monitoring is essential.

medicines.name: Amlodipine (Amlong)
medicines.use: Controls high blood pressure
medicines.dosage: 5mg once daily in the morning as prescribed
medicines.warning: Do not stop suddenly. Monitor BP daily. Report swollen ankles to doctor.

medicines.name: Telmisartan (Telma 40)
medicines.use: Reduces high blood pressure and protects kidneys
medicines.dosage: 40mg once daily as prescribed
medicines.warning: Avoid potassium-rich salt substitutes. Check BP daily.

high blood pressure.disclaimer: BP medications must be taken every day without missing doses. Never adjust dosage without consulting your doctor.

medicines.name: Ispaghula Husk (Isabgol/Metamucil)
medicines.use: Natural fibre supplement to relieve constipation
medicines.dosage: 1 teaspoon in warm water at bedtime
medicines.warning: Drink plenty of water throughout the day. Safe for long-term use.

medicines.name: Lactulose Syrup (Duphalac)
medicines.use: Softens stool and relieves constipation
medicines.dosage: 15ml (1 tablespoon) once or twice daily
medicines.warning: May cause bloating initially. Increase water intake.

constipation.disclaimer: Drink at least 8 glasses of water daily. Eat fruits like papaya, banana, and fibre-rich foods to prevent constipation.

sideEffects: Nausea if taken on empty stomach
precautions: Do not exceed 2g/day in elderly with liver conditions
Avoid alcohol completely while taking
Safe in standard doses – commonly recommended for elderly
Store at room temperature away from moisture
paracetamol.disclaimer: Paracetamol is generally safe for elderly in correct doses. Avoid high doses or prolonged use without doctor supervision.

sideEffects: Vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use
precautions: Never take on empty stomach – always with meals
Stop before surgery or CT scan with contrast dye
Monitor kidney function every 6 months
Avoid alcohol
Carry ID card mentioning you take diabetes medication
metformin.disclaimer: Metformin must only be taken as prescribed. Dosage adjustments needed based on kidney function in elderly.

precautions: Take at same time daily
Stand up slowly to avoid dizziness
Do not stop suddenly without doctor advice
Monitor BP daily at home
Report persistent swelling to doctor
amlodipine.disclaimer: Amlodipine is safe and well-tolerated in elderly. Continue even if you feel well – stopping can cause dangerous BP spikes.

sideEffects: Ringing in ears at high doses
precautions: Always take after food with plenty of water
Avoid if on other blood thinners
Do not use regularly for pain without doctor advice
Tell your dentist/surgeon before procedures
Elderly are at higher risk of GI bleeding
aspirin.disclaimer: Low-dose aspirin for heart protection should only be taken under doctor's advice. Not recommended for routine pain in elderly.

sideEffects: Increased fracture risk with very long-term use
precautions: Take 30 minutes before breakfast for best effect
Do not use for more than 2 weeks without doctor review
Regular users should check magnesium and B12 levels annually
Safe to take with most other medications
omeprazole.disclaimer: Omeprazole is widely used and safe for elderly. Long-term use needs doctor monitoring for nutrient deficiencies.

precautions: Take at bedtime to manage drowsiness
Do not drive after taking
Use lowest effective dose in elderly
Avoid alcohol
Caution in elderly with kidney disease
cetirizine.disclaimer: Cetirizine is effective but causes more drowsiness in elderly. Night-time dosing is strongly recommended.

sideEffects: Hypoglycaemia (low blood sugar) – most important risk
precautions: Always take before breakfast, never skip meal after taking
Keep glucose tablets or sweet biscuits handy at all times
Monitor blood sugar regularly
Avoid alcohol
Watch for symptoms of low sugar: sweating, trembling, confusion
glimepiride.disclaimer: Glimepiride must be used under strict medical supervision. Hypoglycaemia (low sugar) is a serious risk for elderly – never skip meals.

precautions: Do not use potassium supplements or salt substitutes with potassium
Stand up slowly
Monitor kidney function and potassium yearly
Continue even if you feel fine
Avoid in pregnancy
telmisartan.disclaimer: Telmisartan is very effective for BP and kidney protection. Never stop without consulting your doctor.
